title: Latent tyre-state posterior features
summary: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a hidden state with pit stops as resets, which is a better match to strategy decisions than fixed compound life constants or raw cumulative degradation alone.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a hidden state with pit stops as resets, which is a better match to strategy decisions than fixed compound life constants or raw cumulative degradation alone.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight per-race or per-race-compound state-space/Kalman model on lap-time residuals, then emit posterior wear level, wear acceleration, one-step predictive variance, and reset-adjusted residual as new tabular features.
expected_effect: Should make tyre-pressure features less brittle across compounds, wet races, and unusual stint shapes, especially where current fixed thresholds mis-rank edge cases.
risk: Posterior estimates can be noisy around safety cars, traffic, or weather shifts; a poor residual baseline can inject model error rather than signal.
